_comment: Physical-seed exemplar for BrickWallGenerator (facade generator, DQ-e732faee, brick_street_scene_plan node 4): running/stretcher bond wall coursing -- Liam's spec ('the entire logic of the procedural generation based on a physical seed or example'). Coordinates are in the WALL'S OWN 2D UV plane (u=along-wall, v=up-wall) -- BrickWallGenerator maps (u,v) into 3D via the wall's own tangent/up basis, so this file is otherwise IDENTICAL in shape/semantics to BrickPavementGenerator's paver exemplars, just consumed against a vertical plane instead of the ground plane. Real modular brick nominal: 8in x 2-5/8in length x course-height (notes/research/brick_street_construction_methods_2026_07_16.md sec8.2), encoded as brick_length=0.2m / brick_width=0.067m (brick_width here means COURSE HEIGHT for the wall domain -- PhysicalSeedReader's schema calls it the generic 'second physical dimension', reinterpreted per-caller per its own docstring). This is the SAME half-brick-offset-per-course construction as BrickPavementGenerator's running_bond_1brick.json, rescaled to real wall course height instead of paver plan-view width, and is BrickWallGenerator's DEFAULT bond per the 07-14 plan's own choice (running bond for vertical wall facades, sec2.3).
_verification: Single-brick unit cell, lattice_a=(brick_length,0) (repeat along a course), lattice_b=(brick_length/2, brick_width) (each course up shifts by half a brick length) -- the textbook running-bond construction, same proof shape as running_bond_1brick.json (gap-free/overlap-free by construction: an oblique single-member lattice with lattice_a=(L,0) tiles courses with zero horizontal gap, and lattice_b's half-L horizontal component reproduces the standard half-brick stagger with zero vertical gap by construction, not empirically fitted).
